1.commanderBio: Commander Mannion, a 25-year member of the Department, most recently served as the Captain and Executive Officer of the 1st (Central) District, which he will now lead. He brings years of experience within the district to this role. As the Executive Officer, he oversaw daily operations and planned for large-scale events and First Amendment assemblies in the 1st District. Commander Mannion also previously served as the Captain and Executive Officer of the 15th (Austin) District. As a Lieutenant, he was assigned to the Office of the First Deputy Superintendent as the Commanding Officer of Detached Services and as an Administrative Lieutenant. Prior to this, he was a Watch Operations Lieutenant in the 10th (Ogden) District. As a Sergeant, he worked within the Gang Investigation Division and supervised personnel assigned to ATF operational teams and the Cook County Jail Liaison Team. He first worked in the 1st District as a Tactical Sergeant before becoming the Business Liaison Sergeant. As a Sergeant, he also worked in the Targeted Response Unit, the Bureau of Professional Standards and the 10th District. Commander Mannion holds both a master’s degree in educational administration and Foundations and a bachelor’s degree in criminal justice Sciences from Illinois State University.

2.commanderBio: Commander Williams, a 25-year member of the Department, previously served as a Watch Operations Lieutenant in the 002nd (Wentworth) District, which he will now lead. As the Watch Operations Lieutenant, he managed resource allocation and staffing to reduce crime within the district. Commander Williams also worked to reduce violence by creating stronger bonds and partnerships within the community. He was also a Lieutenant in the Office of Constitutional Policing and Reform, where he coordinated in-service training with districts citywide and oversaw tiered deployment. As a Sergeant, Commander Williams worked in the Community Safety Intervention Unit and Special Events. He was also a Sergeant in the 006th (Gresham) District and the 005th (Calumet) District. Commander Williams holds a bachelor’s degree in Criminal Justice from Chicago State University, and attended the Senior Management Institute for Police Training at Boston University in June 2025.

3.commanderBio: I grew up on the south side of Chicago and completed my master’s in education & psychology at Chicago State University. I enjoy traveling, bowling, and watching movies. I’m a 26 year veteran of CPD. My vision for the 3rd district is to partner with community leaders, clergy, and other stakeholders to bridge the gap between the community and the police. I look forward to serving the community.
3.stationAddress: 7040 South Cottage Grove Avenue, Chicago, IL 60637

4.commanderBio: As a 26 year veteran of CPD, the 4th district is definitely where I call home. Not only am I a resident of the district, but this where I have spent most of my career, having served as the tactical lieutenant, a watch commander, a sergeant, and an officer in the district, prior to becoming the district commander. Therefore, I have always had this community’s best interests at heart. I envision working together with the community with an emphasis on accountability, cooperation, and smiling faces. Through this partnership we’ll ensure our community prospers.

5.commanderBio: As a dedicated member of the Department with more than 35 years of service, I remain committed to building trust through strong and lasting community partnerships. Throughout my career as a Captain­Executive Officer in the Bureau of Patrol; as a Lieutenant Commanding Officer and supervising Sergeant within the Homicide, Property Crimes, Special Victim’s Unit and Violent Crimes Sections of the Bureau of Detectives; as a supervising Sergeant in the Bureau of Internal Affairs and Office of the Area One Deputy Chief, including Operation Impact, Project Safe Neighborhoods Federal Gun Task Force, and multiple Gang, Mission, and Tactical Teams I have witnessed firsthand that meaningful relationships are the foundation of effective service and public safety. Therefore, leadership is demonstrated through service, consistency, and sound judgment.Partnership, collaboration, and integrity must be demonstrated through action. Data-driven law enforcement strategies guide our decisions and strengthen our effectiveness. The safety, dignity, and protection of every individual we serve remain our highest priorities. Those under my command are expected to foster positive community engagement through respect, professionalism, and ethical conduct. We recognize that crime reduction is a shared responsibility, and community members are essential partners in building safer, stronger neighborhoods.We are committed to transparency in both action and communication, and to measurable accountability that sustains public trust. Our professionalism, integrity, and courage guide our response to every challenge. Together, we will continue to pursue excellence while building a culture grounded in trust, equity, fairness, and enduring community partnership.One Team, One Fight.

6.commanderBio: Commander Poston, a 26-year member of the Department, most recently served as a Lieutenant in the Public Transportation Section within the Bureau of Counterterrorism. As a Lieutenant, he led public safety operations across the Chicago Transit Authority System, focusing on crime reduction through strategic resource deployment. Commander Poston leveraged the Public Transportation Section’s Strategic Decision Support Center (SDSC) technology to guide the data-driven deployment of resources and support proactive strategies that enhance safety and reduce violence. Before this, he was a Watch Operations Lieutenant and Field Lieutenant in the 002nd (Wentworth) District. As a Sergeant, Commander Poston worked in Airport Operations and the 004th (South Chicago) District. He holds a master’s degree in Management & Financial Fraud from Saint Xavier University and a bachelor’s degree in Law Enforcement Management from Calumet College of St. Joseph. He also attended the Police Executive Research Forum’s Senior Management Institute for Police.

7.commanderBio: The Chicago Police Department offers several services for its Chicago citizens. We have services for purchasing crime and traffic reports, registering your bicycle or camera, reporting and submitting tips, requesting data and statistics, and searching one of our several databases. With over 30+ services we hope to serve your needs.

8.commanderBio: I grew up in the 8th district, just blocks from the police station. I went to Marist High School and completed my master’s degree at Calumet College of St. Joseph. I like to spend my time with my family and my automotive hobby consisting of antique cars and racing. My vision for the 8th district is to build true collaborative partnerships and engage in comprehensive problem-solving with the community in order to reduce violent crime and create and sustain safe communities.

9.commanderBio: Commander Mark is a 26-year member of the Department previously served as a Watch Operations Lieutenant in the 009th (Deering) District, which he now leads. Commander Mark has worked within the Bureau of Patrol for the majority of his career. While in the 009th District, he also served as the Tactical Lieutenant. Prior to his promotion to Lieutenant, Commander Mark was a Watch Sergeant in the Public Transportation Section and the 020th (Lincoln) District. He was also assigned to the 003rd (Grand Crossing) District, where he was a Tactical Sergeant and Watch Sergeant. Commander Mark holds a bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering from the Illinois Institute of Technology.

10.commanderBio: Commander Altamirano, a 22-year member of the Department, previously served as a Lieutenant in the Narcotics Section within the Bureau of Counterterrorism. Commander Altamirano has extensive experience in the Bureau of Counterterrorism, serving as a Sergeant in both the Intelligence Section and the Narcotics Division. As a Sergeant, he also worked on the Area North Gang Enforcement Team and as a Sector Sergeant and Mission Team Supervisor in the 015th (Austin) District. Commander Altamirano holds a bachelor’s degree in Secondary Education from Northeastern University.

11.commanderBio: Commander Tate, a 29-year member of the Department, previously served as a Lieutenant in Area Four Violent Crimes. Prior to this, he was a Lieutenant in the Bureau of Internal Affairs Confidential Investigations Section and a Watch Operations Lieutenant in the 005th (Calumet) District. While a Sergeant, Commander Tate spent more than a decade in the Bureau of Detectives, working on the Investigative Response Team as well as the Area Two Detective Division where he supervised officer involved shooting, homicide, and violent crime investigations. He was also a Sergeant in the 004th (South Chicago) District. Commander Tate holds a master’s degree in Criminal Law from DePaul University, a master’s degree in Law Enforcement Administration from Calumet College of St. Joseph, a master’s degree in Business Administration from St. Xavier University and a bachelor’s degree in Law Enforcement Management from Calumet College of St. Joseph. He is currently pursuing a doctoral degree in Educational Leadership from DePaul University. He also attended the Northwestern University School of Police Staff and Command. Proud to serve as your 11th District Commander. Committed to building trust, keeping our neighborhoods safe, and working hand-in-hand with our community. Every day is a chance to make a difference, as we strive for organizational excellence. Born and raised in Chicago, I’m honored to serve as your Commander. With nearly 30 years of experience in law enforcement, I’m committed to transparency, integrity, and building lasting community trust. From working with youth outreach programs, faith based communities, violence interrupters, business owners, community stakeholders, political partners, to improving neighborhood patrols, every step we take is about making Chicago safer and stronger together. Dedicated to protecting and serving our community with integrity, accountability, empathy, and compassion. Committed to building trust, enhancing public safety, and fostering strong community partnerships.

12.commanderBio: I am a lifelong resident of Chicago. I attended public schools here in the city and so have my children. I enjoy spending time with my family in and around Chicago. My vision is to bring our neighborhoods and communities closer by being inclusive and welcoming, as we work together to reduce crime and increase the feeling of safety for residents, visitors, and commuters. Our residents and business owners can help by being involved through block clubs, attending beat meetings, and joining together to look out for their neighbors as we work together to make the district safer for all. I also hope to encourage residents from all of our very diverse neighborhoods to join our police department and serve as role models for their communities.

14.commanderBio: Commander Rivera, an 18-year member of the Department, previously served as a Lieutenant in the Narcotics Division, where he oversaw narcotics enforcement operations to combat drug related crime and violence. He also served the 006th (Gresham) District as a Watch Operations Lieutenant. During his time as a Sergeant, Commander Rivera worked in multiple areas of the Bureau of Counterterrorism, including the Public Transportation Section Tactical Team, Gang Investigations and Narcotics Investigations. He was also a Sergeant on the Area Four Gang Enforcement team and the 015th (Austin) District Tactical Team. Commander Rivera holds a bachelor’s degree in Public Safety Management from Calumet College of St. Joseph.

15.commanderBio: Commander Morse, a 25-year member of the Department, most recently served as the Captain of the 020th (Lincoln) District. While in the 020th District, Commander Morse assisted in overseeing District operations and providing direction to District personnel. He also served as a Captain and Lieutenant in the Office of Community Policing. While there, he supervised program development aimed at enhancing community engagement and building community trust. Prior to this, he was the Commanding Officer of In-Service Training at the Training Academy. He directed in-service training for LEMART, Tactical Response Unit, Driving School, Procedural Justice and Use of Force Sections. He also served the 003rd (Grand Crossing) District and 002nd (Wentworth) District as a Lieutenant. As a Sergeant, he worked at the Training Academy, the Research & Development Division, the Crime Prevention Information Center and the 003rd District. He was also a Detective in the Bureau of Detectives in Area 1 and Area 4, as well as a Detective in the Bureau of Internal Affairs. Commander Morse holds a master’s degree in Business Administration from Saint Xavier University and a bachelor’s degree in Public Safety Management from Calumet College of St. Joseph.

16.commanderBio: Commander Hanrahan, a 23-year member of the Department, previously served as the Lieutenant in the Forensic Services Division, where he oversaw the processing of all crime scene evidence and evidence submitted to the division. He also previously worked as the Tactical Lieutenant in the 024th (Rogers Park) District. Commander Hanrahan worked in both the Bureau of Detectives and the Bureau of Patrol as a Sergeant. He served as the Violent Crimes Sergeant in the Area Three Detective Division and as a Tactical Sergeant in the 015th (Austin) District. Commander Hanrahan holds a master’s degree in Business Administration from Saint Xavier University and a bachelor’s degree in Law Enforcement Management from Calumet College of St. Joseph.

17.commanderBio: Commander Alvarez, a 24-year member of the Department, most recently served as a Commander in the Bureau of Patrol. Prior to that, he served as a Lieutenant in the 010th (Ogden) District. In this role, he led the District Coordination Officers as part of the Neighborhood Policing Initiative. He has also served as a Lieutenant in the 003rd (Grand Crossing) District. Prior to this, he worked as a 010th District School Sergeant, Field Sergeant and District Station Supervisor. Commander Alvarez holds a master of business administration in financial fraud from Saint Xavier University. He also holds a bachelor’s degree in law enforcement management from Calumet College of St. Joseph.

18.commanderBio: Commander Bielfeldt, a 27-year member of the Department, previously served as Captain of Airport Operations – South, where he oversaw CPD’s public safety operations at Midway Airport. Commander Bielfeldt was also a Captain in the 017th (Albany Park) District, the Central Control Group and the 018th District, which he will now command. While a Lieutenant, he was assigned to the Bureau of Detectives, where he worked on the re-opening of the Area Four and Area Five detective divisions. He was also a Saturation Lieutenant in Area Central, and both a Watch Operations and Field Lieutenant in the 012th (Near West) District. As a Sergeant, Commander Bielfeldt served in the Special Functions Division, the Bureau of Patrol, the 007th (Englewood) District and the 012th District. Commander Bielfeldt holds a master’s degree in Criminal Justice from Lewis University and a bachelor’s degree in Criminal Justice from Illinois State University. He also attended the Northwestern University School of Police Staff and Command.

19.commanderBio: I grew up in the southwest suburbs of Addision and Glendale Heights. I received my Bachelors in Public Safety Management from Calumet College and Masters of Jurisprudence in Criminal Law from DePaul University of Law. My vision is for all community members to feel safe, supported, and proud of the Chicago Police Department. Partnership between police and the community is important to ensure all stakeholders are working together to address issues and challenges. Key to accomplishing this vision is for us to build trust between the police and the community through procedural justice and community involvement.

20.commanderBio: In my 32 years with CPD I’ve worked in many roles, including as one of the founding members of the department’s bicycle patrol, and being certified as an ATF IBIS Firearm Examiner. My vision for the district is to work together with community members in order to reduce crime incidents and create fair, equitable access to all the City of Chicago has to offer its residents and visitors.

22.commanderBio: Commander Darling, a 19-year member of the Department, most recently served as the Lieutenant of the Area 1 Priority Response Team. As the Lieutenant, he oversaw and supervised daily deployments and operations of the team based on the needs of the districts within Area 1. In this role, he also utilized data analysis to inform strategies to address emerging crime trends and community safety concerns. Before this, he was both a Watch Operations Lieutenant and a Field Lieutenant in the 009th (Deering) District. While he was a Sergeant, he also served in the 009th District as a Patrol and Tactical Sergeant. Earlier in his career, Commander Darling was a detective in the Area 1 Detective Division, where he focused on robberies and gained experience in Special Victims and Violent Crimes investigations. Commander Darling holds a bachelor’s degree in Criminal Justice Science from Illinois State University.

24.commanderBio: Commander Christian is a 29-year member of the Department who previously served as the Captain in the 024th (Rogers Park) District, which she has worked for the majority of her career. Commander Christian was a Captain in the 010th (Ogden) District and a Lieutenant in the 011th (Harrison) District. She gained extensive knowledge of the 024th District over the years, having served the district as a Tactical Lieutenant, Watch Operations Lieutenant, Field Lieutenant, Sector Sergeant, District Station Supervisor and Community Policing Sergeant. Commander Christian holds a bachelor’s degree in Public Safety Management from Calumet College of Saint Joseph.

25.commanderBio: My name is Federico Andaverde III, and I am the Commander of the 25th District. I am a Chicago native born and raised on the Southside of Chicago specifically in the Back of the Yards neighborhood. I attended Seward Elementary and Curie High School. After graduating High School, I served four years’ active duty as a United States Marine. I attended calumet College of St. Josephs where I earned by Bachelor’s Degree in Public Safety Management. In the year 2024 I graduated from the F.B.I. National Academy for Law Enforcement executives. My Chicago Police Career began in the 007th District Englewood followed by a few years in Public Housing South, 003rd District, Narcotics, 005th District, 10th District and finally landing in the 25th district. I have 27 years of Police experience in which I have earned over 300 awards including the Lambert Tree Award and Illinois State Medal of Honor. I envision myself helping the community by bringing my vast police knowledge to the officers and community of the 25th district. My plan for Public Safety in the 25th district includes working alongside our civilian counterparts that include Businesses/Community/Faith-based leaders/ and Political Stake holders. I hope this simple strategy helps promote strong relationships between our officers and community while reducing crime.
